mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0 percent threshold, labor market slack triggers recession fears that systematically reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price index indicate robust demand outpacing supply, forcing equity valuations higher over the next month as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a monthly increase in the index of industrial production signals capacity utilization rebound that generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during documented recovery phases, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of monthly observations across a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.0 million units annually, construction sector contraction and reduced household wealth formation trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 65% of quarters where HOUST stays below 1000.0 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate signals real rate compression and loss of central bank credibility, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansions, quarterly real GDP growth above trend accelerates corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.
